channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's inauguration will be first attended by foreign leaders
item.description: For the first time in U.S. history, a president-elect will welcome foreign leaders for one of the most American political traditions — the peaceful transfer of power.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will take hold in much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Convicted NXIVM cult leader's defense accuses FBI of evidence tampering
item.description: Attorneys for convicted NXIVM sex trafficker Keith Raniere say defense experts have uncovered "provable" evidence of tampering in their quest for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 100 Hawaii homeless sent back to mainland families
item.description: "To date, we have sent 137 people from the mainland who are houseless back to their families on the mainland, saving taxpayer costs," John Mizuno said.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok says it's restoring service to US users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: TikTok said Sunday it was restoring service to users in the United States just hours after the popular video-sharing platform went dark in response to a federal ban, which President-elect Donald Trump said he would try to pause by executive order on his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden pardons the late Black nationalist Marcus Garvey as well as 4 others and commutes 2 sentences
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Attorneys for LDS Church appear in federal court, seek to dismiss tithing fraud lawsuit
item.description: Attorneys for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints are seeking to dismiss a lawsuit that could involve millions of members of the church.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: These notable inaugurations weren't held at the US Capitol. Here's why
item.description: For decades, Americans have gathered at the Capitol Building in Washington D.C. to watch the inauguration of the incoming president, with some noteworthy exceptions.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok says it's in the process of restoring service to US users
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States were no longer able to watch or post videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular video-sharing app took effect Sunday amid questions over how long the blackout might last.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These lip balm ingredients may be making your chapped lips worse, experts say
item.description: It's tempting to reach for lip balm during the dry, winter months, but it may not be helping you at all.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Interior Secretary Deb Haaland reflects on tough choices during a historic tenure
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — It started to get real when the artwork was taken down from her office walls. The collection — all creations of Indigenous artists — had been handpicked by U.S.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that’s probably because it has, at least if you’re measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: David Hamilton, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump inauguration on alert for lone wolf attacks: how to spot a bad actor
item.description: An expert weighed in on what law enforcement keep an eye on to spot potential lone wolf attackers during the Inauguration festivities for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Baltimore is saving lives by offering young men resources when they put down the guns
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — With his dad in prison and his mom suffering from alcoholism, Malik Grant faced abandonment and instability early on. He got used to people letting him down.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Legacy news on its heels as second Trump era begins, but not abandoning mission
item.description: It's a rough time for legacy news outlets as Donald Trump prepares to take the presidential oath for a second term.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mahomes and Kelce help Chiefs to a 23-14 win over Texans and another AFC title game trip
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Patrick Mahomes has never lost in the divisional round of the playoffs with the Kansas City Chiefs. The Houston Texans still have never won.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California officials warn against price gouging as rents soar in fire-stricken Los Angeles
item.description: Southern California's expensive housing market is going to get a lot more competitive after deadly firestorms torched more than 12,000 homes and other structures in the Los Angeles area, leaving tens of thousands of people without a place to stay.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wealthy Florida real estate brothers' 'trophies' uncovered as judge denies bail: feds
item.description: Three wealthy brothers are accused of drugging and sexually assaulting dozens of women in multiple states, and prosecutors said new video "trophies" have been seized as evidence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC mayor defends private meeting with incoming President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City's Democratic mayor, who faces federal bribery charges, is defending his private meeting with President-elect Donald Trump as the Republican prepares to retake the White House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eggs are about to get even more expensive, expert says. Here's how much
item.description: Eggs are nearly 40% more expensive now than they were a year ago, the Labor Department said this week. It's about to get even worse, one expert says.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Family of missing pregnant woman reveals suspicions about disappearance
item.description: An Indiana woman, nine months pregnant at the time, disappeared over three months ago, and her family has suspicions about her whereabouts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Need to get a tattoo removed? What to know about the risks, the cost and more
item.description: When you’re young and carefree, a spur-of-the-moment tattoo feels like the ultimate form of self-expression. Years later, though, you might have a change of heart.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: On LA fire lines, inmates shoulder heavy packs and tackle dangerous work for less than $30 a day
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Sal Almanza has worked 24-hour shifts in grueling terrain, cutting fire lines and hauling away brush trying to keep ahead of fires that have devastated several Los Angeles neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia appeals court sides with Trump in affirming dismissal of 6 state charges
item.description: A Georgia appeals court on Friday affirmed the dismissal of six counts in the state's 2020 election interference against President-elect Trump and his allies, upholding a trial judge's decision that the charges must be thrown out due to a lack of detail.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Vermont accused in lawsuit of tracking pregnant women considered unsuitable to be mothers
item.description: Vermont’s child welfare agency used baseless allegations to secretly investigate a pregnant woman and win custody of her daughter before the baby's birth, according to a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Sean Curran? Head of Trump's personal detail to be nominated for Secret Service director
item.description: Sean Curran, the head of President-elect Trump's personal security detail, will be tapped to lead the U.S. Secret Service, according to the president's son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, NewsNation

item.title: Smoke from fire at California lithium battery plant raises concerns about air quality
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fire at the world’s largest battery storage plant in Northern California smoldered Friday after sending plumes of toxic smoke into the atmosphere, leading to the evacuation of up to 1,500 people. The blaze also shook up the young battery storage industry.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homes were burning and roads already jammed when Pacific Palisades evacuation order came, AP finds
item.description: The first evacuation order covering neighborhoods closest to the start of the devastating Pacific Palisades wildfire didn’t come until about 40 minutes after some of those homes were already burning, according to an Associated Press analysis of emergency communications and interviews with survivors.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, GENE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER and AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kevin O'Leary says he's offering $20B cash for TikTok
item.description: Investor Kevin O’Leary, widely known as a star from “Shark Tank,” said he offered TikTok’s owners $20 billion in cash to buy the platform during a Friday appearance on Fox News’s “America’s Newsroom.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: First responders saved a moose that fell through lake ice in New York. Here's how they did it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bull moose that fell through lake ice in the Adirondack Mountains was saved by New York conservation officials in a laborious cold-water rescue. The moose fell through the ice around 11 a.m.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Oakland mayor, longtime partner indicted in alleged bribery scheme
item.description: OAKLAND (AP) — Former Oakland Mayor Sheng Thao, her longtime partner and two local businessmen were named in an indictment unsealed Friday alleging the four conspired to funnel city business to companies owned by the men in exchange for money and benefits to Thao and her partner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Teen MS-13 victim's father slams last-minute Biden DOJ plea deal
item.description: Robert Mickens says no murderers should be granted plea deals, but this one comes as a 'slap in the face' with a suspect who admitted to killing seven.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: Ash and other dangers mean LA area residents who fled fires a week ago won't be going home soon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It has been more than a week since two massive fires forced tens of thousands of people to flee their homes in the Los Angeles area, and officials said Thursday residents won't be going home soon.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9
